GAD shows excess threat-anticipatory responding: BNC210 trial in GAD patients shows amygdala-modulated defensive flight behaviour (PMID 33414442); GAD shows elevated limbic (amygdala/hippocampus/VMPFC/sgACC) resting connectivity predicting attention-to-threat bias (PMID 30035026); antidepressants in GAD modulate amygdala in affect network (PMID 33627201). Sign +1 = release/excess anticipatory threat.

Persistent worry maps to sustained threat dimension; benzodiazepine-sensitive defensive behaviour (PMID 33414442) and sustained limbic hyperconnectivity (PMID 30035026) consistent with chronic threat anticipation.

GAD shows attentional control deficits and attention-to-threat bias (PMID 30035026); mindfulness training partially restores attention (PMID 37359641); meta-analytic data show heterogeneous attention scores with cognitive subdomain decrements (PMID 42191132).

rdoc_subconstruct: Goal Selection; Updating, Representation, and Maintenance
evidence_note: Meta-analysis of longitudinal cognition in GAD reports mixed but generally negative effects on executive subscores partially reversed by CBT (PMID 42191132); mindfulness reduces worry-related goal-maintenance impairment (PMID 37359641).

OCD obsessions trigger anticipatory anxiety; frontoparietal/salience/default-mode network meta-analysis (541 patients) supports threat-monitoring abnormalities (PMID 29410103); HiTOP analysis places OCD with anxiety/fear in shared internalizing dimension (PMID 33636680).
OCD shows broad cognitive-control deficits; frontoparietal network hypoconnectivity (PMID 29410103); stop-signal SSRT deficit (PMID 34968087).

Stop-signal meta-analysis shows OCD has significantly longer SSRT than controls (raw difference 23.43 ms, p<0.001), indicating inhibitory control deficit (PMID 34968087); pediatric OCD neuropsych meta-analysis is more mixed but shows the same direction (PMID 25866081).

OCD shows excess performance monitoring / error signaling: dysconnectivity within and between frontoparietal, default-mode, and salience networks (PMID 29410103); increased ReHo in dorsomedial PFC (PMID 30368070). Sign +1 reflects gain in monitoring/checking drive.
Pediatric OCD meta-analysis reports small interference-control / working-memory effects (PMID 25866081).
Stop-signal reaction time slowing in OCD reflects impaired motor inhibition/termination (PMID 34968087).

Compulsions are conceptualized as released habit-driven motor behaviour; OCD shows aberrant fronto-striatal connectivity supporting habit-system release (PMID 29410103); local fronto-striatal connectivity alterations (PMID 30368070). Sign +1 = release of habit motor output.

Voxel-wise meta-analysis (954 subjects) shows gray matter alterations in right amygdala and para-cingulate gyrus in panic disorder, consistent with acute threat circuitry release (PMID 38765308); antidepressants modulate amygdala in panic patients (PMID 33627201).

Anticipatory anxiety between attacks engages potential-threat circuitry; meta-analytic gray matter changes in cingulate-frontal regions (PMID 38765308); affect-network modulation by treatment (PMID 33627201).

Mini-review of executive function in panic disorder finds selective deficits in attention/set-shifting/decision-making/working memory in a subset of studies; effect is small and inconsistent (PMID 33848752).

Autonomic surges are core to panic attacks; amygdala-prefrontal circuit abnormalities (PMID 38765308) drive autonomic hyperarousal; executive review notes arousal-cognition interactions (PMID 33848752).
Trauma-cue reactivity engages amygdala-mPFC fear circuitry (PMID 36100907); fear loading is a core HiTOP-mapped PTSD dimension (PMID 33581490).

PTSD shows convergent medial PFC structural alterations with widespread frontoparietal/limbic connectivity (PMID 36100907); sustained intrusions, hyperarousal, avoidance are core features (PMID 33581490); meta-analytic neurocognitive review (4108 subjects) confirms chronic threat-related processing changes (PMID 25365762).
Attention/working memory deficit (d=-0.50) in PTSD meta-analysis (PMID 25365762); elderly survivors meta-analysis confirms TMT-A/B and Digit Span deficits (PMID 33490372).

Intrusive memories (re-experiencing) reflect involuntary release/over-retrieval of trauma-encoded declarative memory; re-experiencing is the most psychopathology-saturated PTSD symptom cluster (PMID 33581490); hippocampal-mPFC dysfunction supports this (PMID 36100907). Sign +1 = excess/release.

Voluntary declarative memory is impaired: meta-analysis (4108 subjects) shows large verbal-learning (d=-0.62) and verbal-memory (d=-0.46) deficits (PMID 25365762); elderly-survivor meta-analysis confirms explicit memory deficits (PMID 33490372). Sign -1 captures voluntary retrieval/encoding deficit (paradoxical to intrusion +1).
Executive-function deficits in PTSD documented across multiple test domains in two meta-analyses (PMIDs 25365762; 33490372).
Working-memory deficit (d=-0.50 combined with attention) in PTSD (PMID 25365762); Digit Span backward decrement (PMID 33490372).

Hyperarousal is a defining cluster of PTSD; meta-analytic VBM/FC shows arousal-network alterations (PMID 36100907); hyperarousal correlates with cognitive slowing in 4108-subject meta-analysis (PMID 25365762).
Phasic fear responses to social scrutiny: increased amygdala/globus pallidus activation (PMID 26341469); enlarged P2 amplitudes to threat-related facial expressions (PMID 37315657).

Meta-analyses show SAD patients have amygdala hyperactivation to faces (PMID 26341469); enlarged dorsal striatum gray matter linked to symptom severity (PMID 30140607); threat-related attentional bias to angry faces (PMID 34000291).

Avoidance of social contact and reduced eye-contact behaviour reflects diminished affiliative engagement (PMID 34000291); dorsal striatum gray-matter alterations track social symptom severity (PMID 30140607).

Meta-analyses identify face perception attentional bias and altered ERPs (P1/P2/P3/LPP) (PMID 37315657); eye-tracking shows reduced eye contact with emotional faces (PMID 34000291); altered face-processing network (PMID 26341469). Reception of facial communication is impaired in SAD.
Eye-contact avoidance / hyperscanning observed in SAD reflect production-side facial communication alterations (PMID 34000291).

Activation-likelihood meta-analysis (13 studies, 327 subjects) shows phobic stimuli elicit increased activation in left amygdala/globus pallidus, insula, and thalamus relative to controls; reversed by CBT (PMID 23711114); narrative review confirms right amygdala/ACC hyperactivation reduced after exposure (PMID 23095586).
Anticipatory anxiety toward phobic objects engages potential-threat circuitry; treatment review notes anticipation and avoidance components (PMID 23095586).
Increased thalamus/pulvinar activation to phobic stimuli reflects involuntary attentional capture by feared stimuli (PMID 23711114). Sign +1 = excess threat-attention capture.

Defensive innate motor patterns (freezing, withdrawal) are released by phobic exposure; engagement of basal ganglia/globus pallidus on imaging (PMID 23711114); VR exposure paradigms target same defensive responses (PMID 34173160).

GAD loads strongly on the broad Internalizing spectrum in HiTOP-aligned factor analyses across population samples.

GAD is a prototypical Distress-subfactor disorder within the Internalizing spectrum across multiple HiTOP-aligned IDAS-II validation studies (Spanish PMID 32479335; Romanian PMID 37484097; German PMID 36521138).
OCD loads on the broad Internalizing spectrum across HiTOP factor analyses.

HiTOP-aligned confirmatory factor analyses in acute psychiatric sample (N=2178) place OCD compulsive disorders on the Fear factor (PMID 33636680); IDAS-II three-factor model places OCD on Obsessions/Fear (PMIDs 32479335; 37484097).
Panic disorder loads on the broad Internalizing spectrum.
Panic disorder is a prototypical Fear-subfactor disorder within the Internalizing spectrum; loads on Obsessions/Fear factor of IDAS-II in HiTOP validations (PMIDs 32479335; 37484097).
PTSD loads on the broad Internalizing spectrum.

PTSD re-experiencing and avoidance both robustly relate to Distress dimension; HiTOP-based factor analysis confirms PTSD's strong Distress loading (PMID 33581490); IDAS-II Distress factor (PMID 32479335).
PTSD re-experiencing and avoidance both robustly relate to Fear dimension in HiTOP-aligned analyses (PMID 33581490); IDAS-II Obsessions/Fear factor (PMID 32479335).

Re-experiencing cluster of PTSD relates to Thought Disorder dimension in HiTOP-aligned factor analysis (PMID 33581490); D-PTSD shows altered insula-precuneus/lingual connectivity overlapping with Thought-Disorder phenotype (PMID 31502268).

Dissociative subtype of PTSD presents with depersonalization/derealization (Detachment phenotype); D-PTSD shows distinct insula-frontal connectivity reductions (PMID 31502268); childhood maltreatment predicts D-PTSD detachment symptoms (PMID 32736266).
SAD loads on the broad Internalizing spectrum.
Social anxiety disorder is a prototypical Fear-subfactor (Obsessions/Fear in IDAS-II three-factor model) disorder within Internalizing (PMIDs 32479335; 37484097).
Specific phobia loads on the broad Internalizing spectrum.
Specific phobia is the canonical Fear-subfactor disorder in HiTOP/IDAS-II structures (PMIDs 32479335; 37484097).
